title: Observability
subtitle: Observability & Cost Tracking
description: LLM call tracing, token metering, cost analysis, and structured logging. Without observability, you're flying blind. Track every API call's input, output, latency, and token consumption across multiple providers with unified data structures.
sub_problems: Call tracing: input/output/latency for each LLM invocation
Token metering: precise token consumption accounting per call
Cost analysis: cost breakdown by model, task, and user
Structured logging: queryable structured logs instead of plain text
Alerting: alerts for cost overruns and high error rates
best_practices: Extract token counts precisely from API responses: don't estimate with len(content)/1.5, error margin exceeds 30%
Distinguish 4 token types: input/output/cache_read/cache_write each have different billing rules
Adapt to multi-provider differences: OpenAI lacks cache_write, Anthropic has all 4 types, need unified data structure
Structured logging + JSON persistence: enables programmatic cost distribution and performance bottleneck analysis
Sub-agent log isolation: each sub-agent in multi-agent systems logs in independent sessions

values.MiroThinker-Token: TokenUsage TypedDict extracts precisely from API response
values.PageIndex: Logger passed through function parameters layer-by-layer, no distributed tracing
Custom JsonLogger, full JSON array rewrite to file
Entry records total_page_number + total_token, pipeline steps record intermediate states
No built-in visualization, relies on manual JSON file inspection
tiktoken pre-calculates document token total, no LLM call-level token tracking
Interface supports info/error/debug/exception four levels, but levels not written to data
Full file rewrite on each write, no data loss on process crash

solutions.title: TokenUsage TypedDict four-dimensional tracking with multi-provider adaptation
solutions.description: base_client.py:32-49 defines TokenUsage(TypedDict) unifying 4 token types: total_input_tokens/total_output_tokens/total_cache_read_input_tokens/total_cache_write_input_tokens. openai_client.py _update_token_usage() extracts from usage_data.prompt_tokens/completion_tokens, retrieves cache hits via prompt_tokens_details.cached_tokens (OpenAI lacks cache_write). anthropic_client.py _update_token_usage() extracts all 4 types (includes cache_creation_input_tokens/cache_read_input_tokens). Dual-mode tracking: cumulative + last_call_tokens for single-call records.

solutions.title: TaskLog + StepLog + ColoredFormatter structured task logging
solutions.description: task_logger.py implements two-level structured logging: TaskLog (task-level with task_id/status/steps/total_tokens/sub_agent_sessions) and StepLog (step-level with timestamp/level/title/detail/metadata). Each LLM call, tool execution, and decision point recorded as StepLog. log_step() appends to steps list and console output simultaneously. save() persists to JSON file. ColoredFormatter colors by level (ERROR red/WARNING yellow/INFO green/DEBUG cyan). start_sub_agent_session() creates independent session records for sub-agents.

solutions.title: AOP install_tracing() + TraceCollector lightweight tracing system
solutions.description: Lightweight local LLM call tracing system complementing cloud-based Langfuse. TraceCollector gathers input/output/tokens/duration/model for each LLM call. install_tracing() injects tracing logic via AOP interception with zero intrusion. Supports trace_id correlation across all calls in single task. Integrates with TaskLog, automatically writing trace data to step logs.

solutions.title: Three-layer observability architecture
solutions.description: Multi-agent educational tutoring systems face three-layer observability challenges requiring coordinated tracing across agent boundaries, token accounting across provider APIs, and cost attribution per user session.

solutions.title: LangSmith integration tracing approach
solutions.description: Agent system debugging and optimization face unique challenges requiring distributed tracing across multiple LLM calls, tool invocations, and decision branches with unified context propagation.

solutions.title: Structured JSON logging approach
solutions.description: PDF document structure parsing is a multi-stage LLM-intensive pipeline: TOC detection → TOC extraction → page mapping → accuracy validation → error correction. Each stage involves multiple LLM calls facing observability challenges requiring per-stage token tracking and error attribution.
